entry.title: Washington Post: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia moves to seize Donetsk; widespread missile strikes put Ukraine on alert
  • NATO signs accession protocols for Finland and Sweden as Ukraine faces shelling in east
  • Far from Ukraine, Sri Lanka is the epicenter of a global crisis
  • Peter Brook, towering theater director, dies at 97
  • Chile writes a woke constitution. Are Chileans ready for it?
  • Boris Johnson’s latest scandal prompts resignation of top ministers
  • NATO signs accession protocols for Finland and Sweden
  • Miguel Etchecolatz, enforcer of Argentina’s ‘Dirty War,’ dies at 93
  • Twitter sues Indian government over content removal
  • After years in ISIS prison camp, they now face an uncertain welcome home
  • Activists in Kenya to keep fighting for abortion rights after end of Roe
  • After invasion of Ukraine, a reckoning on Russian influence in Austria
  • Hundreds rescued as Sydney suffers fourth flood in less than 18 months
  • Homes of 85,000 people at risk, but rain eases around Sydney
  • Cairo's historic Nile River houseboats removed in govt push
  • EXPLAINER: Factors behind Sydney's recent flood emergecies
  • Hurricane Bonnie off Mexico briefly season's 1st major storm
  • Caribbean court finds anti-sodomy law unconstitutional
  • Canada to throw out 13.6M doses of AstraZeneca vaccine
  • 2 UN peacekeepers from Egypt were killed, 5 wounded in Mali
  • Mexican navy sets net hooks in bid to help vaquita porpoise
  • Morocco accused of blocking UN visit to Western Sahara
  • As 'Run 3' begins, CERN touts discovery of exotic particles
  • Threats from Highland Park suspect drew police attention in 2019
  • Amid the Highland Park carnage, seven dead and a toddler left alone
  • Highland Park suspect initially charged with 7 counts of murder
  • Highland Park suspect charged with murder as community mourns
  • ‘Nothing feels safe:’ Americans are divided, anxious and quick to panic
  • How do you punish a mass shooter?
  • Hogan orders relaxed rules for Maryland concealed handgun permits
  • Trauma physicians have become depressingly prepared for mass shootings
  • There have been over 300 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • Highland Park shooting: Police arrest ‘person of interest’ after gunman killed 6 at parade
  • Chaos at July Fourth events as fireworks are mistaken for gunshots
  • Their dad was drowning. They saved him using CPR they saw in movie scenes.
  • Georgia grand jury subpoenas Sen. Graham, Giuliani and Trump legal team
  • Post Politics Now: Biden awards Medal of Honor to 4 Vietnam War soldiers
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Crypto lender Vauld offered lifeline a day after suspending withdrawals
  • Oil prices fall sharply as recession worries grow
  • What Europe’s push to simplify chargers means for you
  • Villas at 55-plus Va. community come with lots of amenities
  • How to drain swampy yards and stop wet basements
  • Deck, patio or porch: Which upgrade works best for your outdoor space?
  • Buy Now Pay Later Joins the List of Subprime Losers
  • Boris Johnson’s in Trouble. But the UK Economy’s OK for Now.
  • That Hot Beach Summer Isn’t Looking So Hot
  • Why Volkswagen Buyers Get a Lamborghini for Free
  • Why Payment for Order Flow Made Trades Free But Left SEC Skeptical
  • Biden’s Thorny Options for Changing the Supreme Court
  • Why Saving the Climate Requires a Tough Taxonomy: QuickTake
  • Inflation is even worse than the official numbers suggest
  • What I’ve been up to the last four months
  • This is already a summer I’d like to forget
  • Why crypto will rise again
  • Weaken the filibuster before a wave election? That would be a bad move, Democrats.
  • A labor fight could soon make inflation worse. Will Biden intervene?
  • Danyel Smith’s ‘Very Personal History’ gives Black women of pop music their due
  • Again, Americans are killed by a weapon of war. A ban must be on the table.
  • The Post endorses Angela Alsobrooks for a second term as Prince George’s County Executive
  • Sorry, Mitt Romney. Denial is not an equal-opportunity offense.
  • Gavin Newsom’s TV ad slamming DeSantis fills a void among Democrats
  • The immortal Trump
  • Why this farmer is wary but optimistic about the global food crisis easing
  • The usual suspects
  • Virginia Democrats might pay for rejecting Youngkin’s gas-tax holiday
  • Veterans were opposed to VA health-care changes
  • Are we about to become the Aggregate States of America?
  • Going back in time in Montgomery County schools
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 6 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-06 07:13AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Trick Shots
  • Australia news LIVE: Sydney flood risks grow as PM promises access to disaster payments from tomorrow; Nick Kyrgios accused of assault of ex-girlfriend
  • Bowser blues: No relief in sight for motorists as $2.10 petrol returns
  • Ground Strokes
  • PM declares fourth COVID-19 jab 'inevitable'
  • Hynes tests positive to COVID-19
  • SES video offers first-person view of flood operations
  • How Jacinda Ardern is winning at fashion diplomacy
  • Melbourne theatre’s ‘digital pivot’: pandemic thing or a revolution?
  • This Florida mansion has the ultimate tribute to Star Wars
  • Telstra stops 185 million scam texts reaching customers' phones
  • Man accused of deadly Chicago shooting had planned rampage for weeks, US police say
  • Brooks slams Tigers officials for leaking
  • Gus’ Origin Decider Preview & Top 8 Predictions: Six Tackles with Gus - Episode 19

  • Harold Holt’s disappearance raised at Chris Dawson’s murder trial

  • Zimbabwe introduces gold coins as local currency tumbles

  • Six people killed, dozens injured in fourth of July mass shooting
  • Kangaroo swims through flooded NSW vineyard

  • From the Archives, 1992: Designer defends ‘boring’ new plastic notes
  • Man charged with murder after Qld mother-of-four cold case breakthrough
  • Daniel Johns escapes jail time over drink driving charge
  • Paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon granted bail after ‘newspaper clippings of children’ found in his home

  • Queensland remains unmasked with third COVID peak coming
  • Virgin, Jetstar take aim at unvaccinated ex-employees’ unlawful dismissal claim
  • Frank Dell: downed Mosquito pilot joined Dutch Resistance
  • Qantas ‘can’t afford’ pay rises for engineers, but can it afford a PR war?

  • Qantas engineers threaten strike action in August
  • Energy wars: Russia threatens to cut off Japan’s gas lifeline
  • Warren Buffett-backed Chinese company topples Tesla
  • NRL-themed hotel on the way as deals hit $2b

  • Strata sector robust as business buys offices to occupy
  • ‘Far from short term’: Bubs Australia eyes long-term American dream
  • CBA facing $2b hit from buy now, pay later play Klarna
  • Australians face paying hundreds more in power bills as suppliers pass on rises

  • ‘No, that’ll never work’: Why Courtney Act knocked back the idea for her TV pilot

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-06 07:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong will not return to toughest control rules for arrivals, John Lee says, while also expressing hope to meet Beijing ‘halfway’ on border reopening
  • Hong Kong sectors rush to hold seminars to study Xi Jinping’s speech, sparking debate on effectiveness of such ‘mainland-style’ forums
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee in Legco: city not ‘lying flat’ in pandemic fight, PCR tests can be extended to exempt residents from restrictions
  • New Chief Executive, John Lee, must streamline planning process, boost transitional homes to address Hong Kong’s housing crisis, experts say
  • Hong Kong justice secretary and lawyer groups condemn intimidation of judiciary staff after parcel with white powder sent to High Court
  • 93 Hong Kong students attain perfect score in International Baccalaureate exams, 37 fewer than last year
  • Hong Kong’s premium office vacancy rate hits 19-year high of 12 per cent as more companies adopt work-from-home policies to counter downturn
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong must rein in spread of Omicron subvariants, boost quarantine capacity to help reunite cross-border families, analysts say
  • Hong Kong is now better equipped to deal with Covid-19 and open up to the world
  • Urban Renewal Authority vows to take on even ‘difficult’ projects in Hong Kong’s most crowded areas in Kowloon
  • Hong Kong national security law: children’s books aimed at inciting separatism and hatred towards mainland China, prosecutors say
  • 8 reconstructed dinosaur fossils take over Hong Kong Science Museum for new giant exhibition
  • Remembering Hong Kong theatre legend Ko Tin-lung, who died in his sleep aged 69. ‘He had a great eye for new talent’
  • ‘I will answer any questions’, says new Hong Kong leader John Lee amid criticism of call for queries on specific policy areas in Legco session
  • Black market cigarettes worth HK$80 million seized by Hong Kong customs officers in June blitz on smuggling
  • Hong Kong’s chief executive focuses on keeping public housing waiting time from increasing, but experts says 10,000 more flats needed a year
  • Belarus’s JD.com and Douyin online stores woo Chinese consumers with chocolates and crisps
  • Chinese health officials suspended after refusing to investigate forced removal of couple’s baby
  • ‘Not a joint drill’: Chinese frigate ‘sent to monitor Russian warship’ near Diaoyus
  • Pope hopes deal with China on bishop appointments will be renewed soon
  • Huawei founder Ren Zhengfei seeks employee input on business direction amid struggle to reinvent company under US sanctions
  • US receive over 400 requests to keep China tariffs, Joe Biden still ‘figuring it out’
  • Calling Capstone: Nasa hopeful it can resume contact with spacecraft heading for moon
  • How Nato’s stance on China has changed, and why it matters
  • Chinese scientists try to reduce space junk by using drag sail on rocket
  • China inflation: Beijing ready to tap pork reserves as global price rises hit closer to home
  • Chinese data centre operator GDS sees Singapore as hub for its Southeast Asia expansion plans
  • Top US and Chinese diplomats to meet on sidelines of G20 this week
  • US-China trade: what a tariff removal would mean for Hong Kong firms that have invested overseas
  • India raids offices of Chinese smartphone maker Vivo, after earlier searches on Huawei and Xiaomi
  • Chinese university sanctioned by US declares success of hypersonic test flight
  • China’s new wind tunnel for hypersonic testing is too powerful for the electricity grid: scientists
  • China’s Xiaomi throws down gauntlet to rival Android handset makers, Apple with launch of Leica Camera-equipped smartphones
  • China adds international flights after relaxing Covid-19 controls
  • China records first cases of new Omicron variant BA5
  • Pakistan arrests ‘important accused’ suspect linked to Karachi’s Confucius Institute blast that killed 3 Chinese teachers
  • China and Vietnam vow to talk more but South China Sea tensions – and others – remain
  • Gap between China, South Korea is widening as Seoul pivots to Washington
  • China pledges to share more data on the Mekong River with downstream nations
  • Japanese grandpa turns accidental YouTube star with ‘warm and peaceful’ art tutorials
  • Indonesia’s first foot-and-mouth outbreak in 36 years threatens to upend Eid ul-Adha celebrations
  • Japan scientists clone freeze-dried mice in bid to beat extinction
  • South China Sea: Philippines’ Marcos wants ‘to resolve the conflicts’ with Beijing, says he’s open to working with China’s military
  • Will an Upper House election win allow the LDP to amend Japan’s pacifist constitution?
  • Muslim musicians in Kashmir test India’s limits on free speech through ‘conscious music’
  • Australia floods force thousands more to flee from country’s largest city of Sydney
  • Philippine children of uniformed officers targeted as ‘easy prey’ by communists seeking to infiltrate security agencies
  • Coronavirus: Singapore health minister says current Covid-19 wave ‘will not be as severe’ as earlier Omicron outbreak
  • UK newspapers: ‘Game over’ for Boris Johnson after key ministers quit
  • Parents of boy, 2, found alone at US July 4 parade shooting among dead
  • CERN scientists observe 3 ‘exotic’ new particles as Large Hadron Collider comes back online
  • Rudy Giuliani’s testimony sought in Donald Trump election probe
  • UK’s Boris Johnson on the brink as key ministers quit
  • July 4 shooter planned attack for weeks, wore women’s clothing to escape, police say
  • UK PM Boris Johnson ‘forgot’ about groping complaints against MP
  • Ukraine war: at least two people killed after ‘massive’ Russian shelling in city of Sloviansk: mayor
  • Spanish fighter jet intercepted an easyJet flight after an 18-year-old passenger faked a bomb threat on social media
  • Ukraine war: Nato formally launches ratification process for Sweden, Finland membership, amid Türkiye’s veto threat
  • Joe Biden tries to sound upbeat on July 4, despite 309 US mass shootings this year
  • China Huadian’s renewable energy unit files for US$4.5 billion Shanghai IPO to fund expansion of solar and wind capacity
  • China is in no danger of losing its supply chain supremacy to India or Vietnam
  • Chinese developer Fantasia gets reprieve from creditors on US$108 million bond repayment
  • Hong Kong stocks weaken as mass Covid-19 testing reignites China lockdown fears while Alibaba gains on price upgrades
  • Shanghai’s home rental market suffers as owners struggle to replace tenants who fled town and displaced workers during lockdown crisis
  • How lonely is China’s Gen Z? Tencent-backed social networking app Soul poses the question as it files for Hong Kong IPO
  • What a strong dollar means for foreign investors in China
  • How geopolitics and Fed policies are putting US external sustainability at risk
  • China’s BYD takes Tesla’s crown as the world’s biggest electric vehicle producer by sales
  • Hong Kong’s Hysan Development, Swiss flexible office giant IWG eye expansion of their co-working spaces in the Greater Bay Area
  • China Evergrande’s EV unit to start taking orders for Hengchi SUV just over a week after embattled developer receives wind-up petition
  • NetEase’s William Ding and Xiaomi’s Lei Jun relinquish corporate roles at video gaming entities amid tightened regulation
  • Hong Kong will attract a new generation of expats, but quarantine restrictions must go first, says Link Reit’s Nicholas Allen
  • Hong Kong house gets an Asian holiday home vibe, with Hermès-inspired colours and warm wood all over
  • Kang So-yeon from Netflix show Single’s Inferno on a K-pop return, her new song Loca Loca and her 15 years chasing an entertainment career
  • Why sunscreen for black and brown-skinned people is vital, and which formulas work best
  • Taika Waititi, Thor: Love and Thunder director also known for Jojo Rabbit and Hunt for the Wilderpeople, is a Hollywood powerhouse
  • Thor: Love and Thunder movie review – Chris Hemsworth and Natalie Portman reunite for a planet-hopping Marvel romance
  • Moderate drinkers more likely to indulge in binge drinking – many consume a week’s worth of alcohol on a night out with friends or at weekends
  • Why playing StarCraft is like running a business and how the video game changed a tech firm founder’s life
  • Giambattista Valli’s 10th anniversary in haute couture at Paris Fashion Week: the autumn/winter 2022-23 collection was all colourful tulle dresses and shimmering body suits
  • Who is Gautam Adani’s wife, Priti? The spouse of Asia’s new richest man fronts the charitable Adani Foundation, is a qualified dentist, and loves gardening and reading about start-ups
  • Meet Prince Christian of Denmark, son of Crown Prince Frederik and Princess Mary – his parents just pulled the 16-year-old out of Herlufsholm School due to shocking revelations
  • Who is the richest Kardashian-Jenner beau? Net worths, ranked – from Kim’s BF Pete Davidson’s SNL earnings, to Kylie’s baby daddy Travis Scott and Kourtney’s husband Travis Barker’s music millions
  • How did LeBron James become the first active NBA billionaire? The Lakers and Space Jam star made it with a little help from Adele’s boyfriend Rich Paul … and a lifetime deal with Nike
  • 9 male celebrities dating – or married to – older women, from Harry Styles and Olivia Wilde, and Jennifer Lopez and Ben Affleck, to Britney Spears and Sam Asghari who just had an LA wedding
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Dior paid homage to Ukraine with an embroidery-rich autumn/winter 2022-23 collection by Maria Grazia Chiuri, riffing on Eastern European designs
  • 5 challenges Twice’s Nayeon faced before her solo debut, chart-topping ‘Im Nayeon’: a K-pop bullying scandal, an Instagram ‘ban’ … but did JYP really force her to sing all the high notes?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live news: Donetsk governor urges evacuations amid Russian offensive

  • Conjuring Kesha: the pop star’s ghost-hunting show is a bizarre blast

  • Families sue TikTok after girls died while trying ‘blackout challenge’

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
